Icon Labeler — manual test page

Load the unpacked extension, then open this file. With the debug badge on you should see dashed outlines + labels on exactly the icons marked SHOULD label and nothing on the should NOT touch ones. Then navigate with a screen reader and confirm the announcements.

On-demand (ephemeral) mode — the default. Nothing is written to the DOM at load. Tab through the page: the focusable icon controls in Section 3 (icon-only button and link) should announce their generated name as they receive focus (e.g. "remove, button"), and the aria-label is removed again on blur — inspect an element while focused vs. blurred to see it appear and vanish. Non-focusable standalone icons (Sections 1, 5, 6, 8) are NOT labeled in this mode by design; switch to legacy "label at load" mode in the options page to see those.

1. Bare inline SVG — SHOULD label

No accessible name anywhere. The core case.

2. Icon inside an already-named button — should NOT touch

Double-speak guard. The button already has a name.

3. Icon-only button, no other name — SHOULD label

The icon must supply the button's accessible name.

4. Author already made it accessible — should NOT touch

Has aria-label / a <title> / aria-hidden.

5. Sprite <use> reference — SHOULD label

Wrapping <svg> gets the label; geometry resolved from the symbol.

6. <img> of an SVG — SHOULD label (alt)

Data-URI SVG with no alt → we set alt.

7. Dynamically inserted (SPA) — SHOULD label

Inserted 1.5s after load to exercise the MutationObserver.

8. Inside an open shadow root — SHOULD label

Design-system pattern. Closed roots are unreachable (documented).
